bets.reasoning: Heat near even money at 1.98 vs Pistons — historically resilient franchise under Spoelstra; competitive matchup for both teams fighting for playoff position
Kings at even money (2.00) offers excellent value; De'Aaron Fox and Domantas Sabonis-led offense capable of winning at home; road Bulls at 1.70 seems overpriced
Suns at 2.42 looks undervalued for a talent-rich roster; speculative play on market overreaction to recent Suns struggles; home court advantage

entries.summary: Opening bets — 3 picks totalling €400. ALL 3 WON! Heat 121-110 Pistons, Kings 126-110 Bulls, Suns 111-99 Hornets. Net P&L: +€439. Bankroll: €1000 → €1439.

bets.reasoning: 76ers are catastrophically undermanned: Embiid OUT (oblique), Paul George OUT (suspension), Maxey OUT (finger) — their top 3 players all absent. Cavaliers have Mitchell back from injury and are at near full strength. Home court advantage. Spread opened at -10.5, moved to -12.5 on injury news. At 1.91 for -12.5, market still offers good value for a decimated opponent.

bets.match: Oklahoma City Thunder vs Denver Nuggets
bets.reasoning: OKC is severely depleted: Jalen Williams OUT (hamstring), Isaiah Hartenstein OUT (calf), Alex Caruso QUESTIONABLE (hip), Chet Holmgren QUESTIONABLE (illness). Jokic confirmed playing despite minute restriction. Getting +7.5 against a potentially 4-man-down OKC with Jokic available is excellent spread value. Jamal Murray confirmed available at tip-off.

Michael Porter Jr. confirmed OUT for the Nets (their leading scorer). Grizzlies are the more complete team even without Ja Morant — better depth, better coaching, better defensive intensity. Nets sitting near bottom of East with no playoff ambition — potential tanking risk. Value at 1.75 for the superior roster.

entries.summary: Day 2 settle — CLE ✅ covered -12.5 (115-101, margin +14), DEN ✅ covered +7.5 (OKC 129-126, lost by only 3), MEM ❌ ML LOST (BKN 126-115, Nets bench explosion). Day 2 net P&L: +€308.25. Total P&L: +€747.25. Bankroll: €649 → €1,747.25.

bets.match: San Antonio Spurs vs Boston Celtics
bets.reasoning: Spurs are 47-17, best record in the NBA — won 15 of last 16, 12-4 ATS in last 16 games. Celtics missing Vucevic (OUT, finger surgery) and Tatum limited to ~27-min restriction (only 3rd game back from Achilles tear). Victor Wembanyama dominant at home — Spurs just demolished Houston 145-120. Depleted Celtics frontcourt vs Wembanyama spells trouble covering the spread. Spurs home court (Frost Bank Center) — commanding 33-6 home record this season.

bets.match: Los Angeles Lakers vs Minnesota Timberwolves
bets.reasoning: LeBron James confirmed OUT for 3rd straight game (left foot arthritis + hip contusion). Luka Doncic (32.5 ppg, #1 scorer in NBA) at home on NBC primetime — capable of carrying the Lakers on a big-stage night. Lakers home court at Crypto.com Arena — crowd factor in a marquee TV game. Market prices Lakers at ~49% implied probability (2.05); marginal value given Luka's ceiling at home.

bets.match: Chicago Bulls vs Golden State Warriors
bets.reasoning: Warriors are on the second leg of a back-to-back (played Jazz in Utah on Monday, 32+ min from key players). Draymond Green listed as QUESTIONABLE (injury management, lower back). Bulls arrive rested and already acclimated to the West Coast. Line has barely moved suggesting market is not fully pricing in Warriors fatigue. Getting +6.5 on a rested team against a fatigued, depleted opponent is sharp value.

entries.summary: Day 3 commit — 3 picks totalling €665 (38% of bankroll). 2 from 14:00 draft (Spurs -3.5, Lakers ML) + 1 new late pick (Bulls +6.5 off Warriors back-to-back). No cancellations. Bankroll: €1,747.25 → €1,082.25. Potential return if all win: +€1,260.00.
